content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach reaches for AoA-flavored moves — refusing to fully validate the trap, gently questioning 'nothing to be done about it,' and surfacing the protective function of the structural framing — and the timing on several of these is good (turns 6, 12, 14 land at moments the client can receive them, evidenced by the client's pauses and admissions). However, the moves stay primarily cognitive/insight-oriented rather than moving toward feeling. When the client opens (the small laugh in 9, the 'not what was supposed to happen' in 11, the long pause in 15), the coach reflects and reframes but does not invite him into the feeling underneath (helplessness, shame, grief about the imagined life). The 'what would you say to a friend' move in 12 is a generic therapy move, not an AoA move, and it pulls the client further into analysis. The coach also does a fair amount of strategizing-adjacent inquiry ('what has looking actually looked like') which risks colluding with the problem-frame. The hit rate is roughly half: good refusal-to-collude work, but the sequence skips the feeling step and heads toward agency/insight, which is why this sits at 2 rather than 3.",
  "evidence_turns": [6, 8, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reflects frustration and offers a useful either/or distinction (structural vs. feeling-of-invisibility). Reasonable opening read, though the framing invites cognitive sorting rather than contact."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Picks up the charged phrase 'best years disappearing' — good read and good move toward what's underneath the complaint."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refusal-to-fully-validate-the-trap move, delivered warmly: 'do you actually believe that?' Well-timed; client softens and admits 'frustration, probably.'"},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Pivots to investigating the search behavior. This is closer to strategizing the external situation than staying with the live material, and it moves away from the opening the client just made."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Catches the small laugh — good read — but the follow-up question stays in self-evaluation territory rather than inviting the feeling underneath the self-judgment."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Right moment for a move toward feeling (client just said 'it's not what was supposed to happen' with a pause), but coach goes into reframe-plus-'what would you say to a friend,' a generic move that keeps things cognitive. Missed opportunity to invite grief or helplessness."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Strong naming of the protective function of the structural story — this is the closest the coach gets to 'i-dont-buy-your-story.' Good timing, lands well per the client's long pause."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client arrives at the real avoidance ('easier than finding out the real answer') largely under his own steam; whether the coach can now move toward the feared feeling is untested."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["i-dont-buy-your-story"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary", "fight-or-roll-over-binary", "aggressive-victim-stance", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome", "fear-triangle"],
  "notes": "Coach avoids the major traps (doesn't validate the trap, doesn't strategize HR, doesn't offer false reassurance) but stops short of inviting the feared feeling (helplessness/shame) underneath the structural story. The work is solid at the level of insight but does not complete the feeling-before-action sequence."
}
